key_concepts: Biodiversity and Need for classification
Three domains of life, taxonomy and systematics
Concept of species and taxonomical hierarchy
Binomial nomenclature
Five kingdom classification system
Salient features of Monera, Protista and Fungi
Classification into major groups with examples
Lichens, Viruses and Viroids
Classification of plants into major groups
Salient and distinguishing features of Algae, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta, Gymnospermae
Features and examples of Angiosperms
Salient features and classification of non-chordates up to phyla level
Classification of chordates upto class level
Examples for each category

units.unit_name: Structural Organization in Plants and Animals

key_concepts: Morphology of root, stem and leaf
Inflorescence types
Structure of flower, fruit and seed
Description of family Solanaceae
Tissue systems in dicots and monocots
Structure and functions of tissue systems
Morphology and Anatomy of frog systems
Functions of digestive, circulatory, respiratory, nervous and reproductive systems
Cell theory and cell as basic unit of life
Structure of prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells (Plant vs Animal)
Cell envelope, membrane and wall structure
Organelles: Endomembrane system, ER, Golgi, Lysosomes, Vacuoles, Mitochondria, Ribosomes, Plastids, Microbodies
Chemical constituents: Proteins, Carbohydrates, Lipids, Nucleic acids (structure and function)
Enzyme types, properties and action
Phases of cell cycle
Mitosis and its significance
Meiosis and its significance
Autotrophic nutrition and site of photosynthesis
Pigments involved (elementary idea)
Photochemical and biosynthetic phases, cyclic/non-cyclic photophosphorylation
Chemiosmotic hypothesis, Photorespiration, C3 and C4 pathways
Exchange of gases and cellular respiration (Glycolysis, Fermentation)
TCA cycle and Electron Transport System
Energy relations: ATP generation count
Amphibolic pathways and Respiratory Quotient
Seed germination, phases of growth and growth rate
Conditions for differentiation, dedifferentiation and redifferentiation
Plant growth regulators: Auxin, Gibberellin, Cytokinin, Ethylene, ABA

Mendelian inheritance, deviations from Mendelism (incomplete dominance, co-dominance), multiple alleles and blood groups, pleiotropy, polygenic inheritance, chromosome theory of inheritance, sex determination mechanisms
Linkage and crossing over, sex-linked inheritance (haemophilia, colour blindness)
Mendelian disorders in humans (thalassemia), chromosomal disorders (Down's syndrome, Turner's, Klinefelter's syndromes)
Search for genetic material, DNA as genetic material; Structure and packaging of DNA/RNA
DNA replication, Central Dogma (transcription, translation), Genetic code
Gene expression and regulation (lac operon)
Genome projects (Human and rice genome) and DNA fingerprinting
Origin of life, evidences for biological evolution (paleontology, comparative anatomy, embryology, molecular)
Darwin's contribution and modern synthetic theory of evolution
Mechanism of evolution: variation through mutation and recombination
Digestion and absorption processes (alimentary canal, enzymes, hormones)
Nutritional disorders: PEM, indigestion, constipation, vomiting, jaundice, diarrhea
Role of microbes in human welfare
